articles.title: Lululemon shares drop as new CEO appointment leaves investors unimpressed
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica's shares dropped 4% after naming former Nike executive Heidi O'Neill as its new CEO. Investors and analysts were reportedly unimpressed with the appointment, preferring activist investor Elliott Investment Management's choice of retail veteran Jane Nielsen. This comes as Lululemon navigates weak sales, increased competition, and an ongoing proxy fight with its founder, Chip Wilson.

This comes as Lululemon navigates weak sales, increased competition, and an ongoing proxy fight with its founder, Chip Wilson.

articles.title: Tim Cook May Have Bought Nike Stock, But The Sneaker Giant Faces Potential 'Generational Reset,' Says This Analyst
articles.summary: Nike Inc. is experiencing one of its most significant stock drawdowns, leading analysts to suggest a potential "generational reset" for the brand. Despite this, insider investments, including purchases by Apple CEO Tim Cook and Nike CEO Elliott Hill, signal confidence in the company's long-term value. While Nike beat revenue and earnings expectations in its recent fiscal third quarter, a weak fourth-quarter sales forecast negatively impacted its stock price and led to lowered analyst target prices.
articles.text_to_analyze: Tim Cook May Have Bought Nike Stock, But The Sneaker Giant Faces Potential 'Generational Reset,' Says This Analyst. Nike Inc. is experiencing one of its most significant stock drawdowns, leading analysts to suggest a potential "generational reset" for the brand. Despite this, insider investments, including purchases by Apple CEO Tim Cook and Nike CEO Elliott Hill, signal confidence in the company's long-term value. While Nike beat revenue and earnings expectations in its recent fiscal third quarter, a weak fourth-quarter sales forecast negatively impacted its stock price and led to lowered analyst target prices.

articles.title: Former Nike executive to take over as Lululemon's new CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O'Neill as its new CEO, effective September 8. This decision comes after months of shareholder pressure and leadership changes, following a turbulent period for the athleisure retailer. O'Neill's experience at Nike is expected to help Lululemon address declining share prices, increased competition, and the need for product innovation.

articles.title: Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8. This decision comes after months of criticism regarding the company's management and performance, including pressure from founder Chip Wilson. O'Neill is expected to lead the athleisure retailer through increased competition and efforts to revitalize its product innovation and market presence.

articles.title: Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8th, amidst months of internal and external pressure regarding the company's leadership and performance. O'Neill's extensive experience at Nike, where she significantly contributed to its growth, is expected to help Lululemon address mounting competition and revitalize its brand. Her appointment follows the departure of former CEO Calvin McDonald and a period with interim co-CEOs.

articles.title: Lululemon names former Nike executive O’Neill its next CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Heidi O’Neill, former Nike Inc. president of consumer, product and brand, as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8. O'Neill takes the helm during a challenging period for Lululemon, marked by slowing growth, product issues, and investor concerns, including a significant stake by Elliott Investment Management and public criticism from founder Chip Wilson. Her appointment comes after previous CEO Calvin McDonald's departure in January.

articles.title: Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Heidi O'Neill, a former Nike executive, as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8th. O'Neill will also join the company's board of directors. This change comes after shareholder criticism regarding Lululemon's share price and sales performance, following the previous CEO's resignation in December.

articles.title: Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its next chief executive officer, effective September 8. This decision comes after months of criticism regarding the company's management and performance, including calls from estranged founder Chip Wilson for new board members. O’Neill, credited with helping Nike grow significantly during her 25-year tenure, will also join Lululemon's board.

articles.title: NIKE (NKE) CEO boosts stake with April open-market share buy
articles.summary: NIKE, Inc.'s President & CEO, Elliott Hill, acquired 23,660.235 shares of Class B Common Stock on April 13, 2026, at a weighted average price of $42.27 per share. This open-market purchase increased his direct holdings to 265,247.235 NIKE Class B shares. The amended Form 4/A was filed to clarify the weighted average price resulting from multiple trades.
articles.text_to_analyze: NIKE (NKE) CEO boosts stake with April open-market share buy. NIKE, Inc.'s President & CEO, Elliott Hill, acquired 23,660.235 shares of Class B Common Stock on April 13, 2026, at a weighted average price of $42.27 per share. This open-market purchase increased his direct holdings to 265,247.235 NIKE Class B shares. The amended Form 4/A was filed to clarify the weighted average price resulting from multiple trades.

articles.title: Lululemon says it picks former Nike executive as next CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica has named Heidi O'Neill, a former top Nike executive, as its new chief executive, replacing Calvin McDonald. The appointment comes as the athletic apparel retailer faces declining sales and pressure from investors to revitalize its business. Lululemon's stock price has fallen by 38% in the past year due to competition and shifts in consumer preferences.

articles.title: Lululemon Names Former Nike Executive O’Neill Its Next CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Heidi O’Neill, formerly Nike Inc.'s president of consumer, product and brand, as its new chief executive officer. O'Neill will assume the role on September 8, succeeding Calvin McDonald who departed in January. This leadership change comes as the athletic retailer aims to overcome a period of slowing growth and investor concerns.

articles.title: Lululemon appoints long-time Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as new CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its new CEO, effective Sept. 8, following a four-month search. Ms. O’Neill will be tasked with reinvigorating the brand amidst declining stock prices, competition, and concerns about stagnation, particularly in North America where sales have been faltering. Her extensive experience at Nike and commitment to product innovation and cultural relevance are expected to address Lululemon's current challenges and drive global growth.

Her extensive experience at Nike and commitment to product innovation and cultural relevance are expected to address Lululemon's current challenges and drive global growth.

articles.title: Nike veteran Heidi O’Neill will take over as lululemon CEO
articles.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. (NASDAQ: LULU) has appointed Heidi O’Neill, a seasoned Nike veteran, as its new Chief Executive Officer, effective September 8, 2026. O'Neill brings over three decades of experience in the performance apparel, footwear, and sport industry, having previously served in various leadership roles at Nike, where she played a key role in growing the company. Interim co-CEOs Meghan Frank and André Maestrini will continue to lead until O’Neill’s arrival, after which they will return to their previous senior leadership positions.

Interim co-CEOs Meghan Frank and André Maestrini will continue to lead until O’Neill’s arrival, after which they will return to their previous senior leadership positions.
